program_outcomes: Matematik, fen bilimleri ve ilgili mühendislik disiplinine özgü konularda yeterli bilgi birikimi; bu alanlardaki kuramsal ve uygulamalı bilgileri, karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinde kullanabilme becerisi.
Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerini saptama, tanımlama, formüle etme ve çözme becerisi; bu amaçla uygun analiz ve modelleme yöntemlerini seçme ve uygulama becerisi.

Karmaşık bir sistemi, süreci, cihazı veya ürünü gerçekçi kısıtlar ve koşullar altında, belirli gereksinimleri karşılayacak şekilde tasarlama becerisi; bu amaçla modern tasarım yöntemlerini uygulama becerisi.

Mühendislik uygulamalarında karşılaşılan karmaşık problemlerin analizi ve çözümü için gerekli olan modern teknik ve araçları geliştirme, seçme ve kullanma becerisi; bilişim teknolojilerini etkin bir şekilde kullanma becerisi.
Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin veya disipline özgü araştırma konularının incelenmesi için deney tasarlama, deney yapma, veri toplama, sonuçları analiz etme ve yorumlama becerisi.
Disiplin içi ve çok disiplinli takımlarda etkin biçimde çalışabilme becerisi; bireysel çalışma becerisi.

Türkçe sözlü ve yazılı etkin iletişim kurma becerisi; en az bir yabancı dil bilgisi; etkin rapor yazma ve yazılı raporları anlama, tasarım ve üretim raporları hazırlayabilme, etkin sunum yapabilme, açık ve anlaşılır talimat verme ve alma becerisi.
Yaşam boyu öğrenmenin gerekliliği bilinci; bilgiye erişebilme, bilim ve teknolojideki gelişmeleri izleme ve kendini sürekli yenileme becerisi.
Etik ilkelerine uygun davranma, mesleki ve etik sorumluluk bilinci; mühendislik uygulamalarında kullanılan standartlar hakkında bilgi.
Proje yönetimi, risk yönetimi ve değişiklik yönetimi gibi, iş hayatındaki uygulamalar hakkında bilgi; girişimcilik, yenilikçilik hakkında farkındalık; sürdürülebilir kalkınma hakkında bilgi.

Mühendislik uygulamalarının evrensel ve toplumsal boyutlarda sağlık, çevre ve güvenlik üzerindeki etkileri ve çağın mühendislik alanına yansıyan sorunları hakkında bilgi; mühendislik çözümlerinin hukuksal sonuçları konusunda farkındalık.

courses.name: Concepts in Computer Engineering
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop a common understanding of computer science, strengthen students' ability to describe, analyze, and solve problems, and introduce algorithmic thinking and design at a higher level. It also provides basic knowledge of computer architecture, programming languages, data storage, software engineering, computer networks, and the application areas of computers.
courses.description: Basic computer knowledge covering the history of computing, terms and definitions, system components and processes, computer architecture, data storage, computer networks and internet, software engineering, problem solving and algorithm design, data structures, and application fields of computers.
learning_outcomes: To learn about the history of computing
To learn the principle concepts and definitions
To have the ability to define, analyze and solve problems
To have the habitude of algorithmic thinking
To learn design of algorithms
To have principle knowledge about the computer architecture
To have principle knowledge about programming languages and familiarity of different programming languages
To have basic knowledge about different data storage methods
To have general knowledge about software engineering concepts
To learn the principles of computer networks
To learn the application fields of the computers
resources: J. G. Brookshear. Computer Science An Overview. 7th Ed. 2002. Addison-Wesley.
G. Michael Schneider, Judith L. Gersting. An Invitation to Computer Science. 1st Ed. West Publishing Company, USA.
George Beekman, Micheal J. Quinn. Computer Confluence. 7th Ed. Prentice Hall.

courses.name: Development of Reading and Writing Skills I
Programming Basics
courses.purpose: To give the students the fundamentals of computer programming and introduce basic concepts of algorithmics and algorithmic terminologies.
courses.description: Fundamentals of computer programming: sequence, decision, repetition, syntax, compilation, debugging and maintenance, procedures, parameters, arrays, object, top-down structured design, layout and style. The emphasis is on an engineering “right-first-time” approach to solving large problems using computers. Basic concepts of algorithmics and algorithmic terminologies.
learning_outcomes: To be able to provide algorithmic solutions for basic problems.
To be able to use and explain main concepts of programming.
To be able to design and apply decision structures.
To be able to design and apply loop structures.
To be able to monitor and analyze computer software.
To be able to design, define and use functions.
To be able to use recursive approach for problem solving.
To be able to design, define and use class.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Programming Basics – Tutorial – I
Hafta 2: Introduction to Programming Basics – Tutorial – II
resources: Starting Out with Python, Tony Gaddis
The Practice of Computing Using Python, Punch & Enbody, Global Ed.
Python How to Program, Deitel & Deitel
Think Python: How to Think Like a Computer Scientist by Allen B

courses.name: Data Structures
courses.purpose: To improve the students understanding and abilities related to data structures and algorithms. To improve the students ability to program.
courses.description: Basic concepts of data, data structures and data types: arrays, strings, linear structures, sequential searching and sorting techniques, stacks, queues, pointers, linked lists. Various forms of m-way search and B-trees.
learning_outcomes: To be able to use and design abstract data types.
To be able to implement array and linked data structures.
To be able to design and analyze recursive data structures and algorithms.
To be able to implement and use algorithms and data structures for searching.
weekly_topics: Hafta 5: Recursive Data Structures: Linked Lists and Trees
Hafta 6: Elementary Sorts: Selection Sort, Insertion Sort, and Shellsort
Hafta 10: Symbol Tables and Binary Search
resources: Data Structures and Abstractions with Java, F.M. Carrano and T.M. Henry, 4th ed., Pearson, 2016.
Goodrich, M. T., Tamassia, R., & Goldwasser, M. H. (2013). Data structures and algorithms in Python. John Wiley & Sons Ltd.

courses.name: Discrete Structures
courses.purpose: To teach the problem solving strategies, techniques, and tools. To provide students with the ability of logical and mathematical thinking. To form the background related to the discrete mathematics concepts and the corresponding mathematical notations. To develop the critical thinking ability by introducing algorithmic solutions to some abstract problems.
courses.description: Logic, propositions and proof methods. Sets. Functions, sequences and summations. Algorithms and complexity analysis. Integers and division. Matrices. Mathematical induction. Recursive definitions and algorithms. Probability theory. Counting techniques. Graphs. Trees.
learning_outcomes: To be able to compare and use different discrete structures
To be able to analyze problems and identify appropriate solution schemes
To be able to demonstrate abstraction ability
weekly_topics: Hafta 10: Counting, pigeonhole principle, permutation, and combination
resources: Zeitz, Paul; 1999; “The Art and Craft of Problem Solving”; John Wiley & Sons, Inc.
Rosen, Kenneth H.; 2007; “Discrete Mathematics and its Applications”; 6th Ed.; McGraw-Hill
Stein, Clifford, Drysdale, Robert L., Bogart, Kenneth; 2011; “Discrete Mathematics for Computer Scientists”; Pearson

courses.name: Development of Reading and Writing Skills II
Programming Fundamentals
courses.purpose: To provide students with the ability of object oriented programming. To teach object oriented software design at beginners level. To teach object oriented coding of fundamental algorithms and data structures. To teach Java API definition and use at intermediate level. To provide students with the ability of simulation programming.
courses.description: Control Structures, Classes and Methods, Inheritance, Polymorphism, Interfaces, Exception Handling, Streams and File I/O, Recursion, Dynamic Data Structures and Generics.
learning_outcomes: Apply the operations for maintaining common data structures in object-oriented way
Design algorithms in object-oriented way for solving computing problems
Know and compare different programming approaches
Analyze problems, design and implement solutions
Demonstrate the ability to abstract in object oriented context
Apply computer engineering methods to real world problems
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Object Oriented Programming
Hafta 2: Control structures and data storage
Hafta 11: Object oriented analysis and design
Hafta 12: Design and code with UML class diagrams
Hafta 14: Repetition of important topics and evaluation of the period
resources: Walter Savitch; 2012; “Java: An Introduction to Problem Solving and Programming”; 6th Ed.; Upper Saddle River, NJ: Prentice-Hall; ISBN 0-273-75142-5
Deitel&Deitel; “Java How to Program”; 9th Ed.; Upper Saddle River, NJ: Prentice-Hall; ISBN 0-273-75976-0

courses.name: Theory of Computation
courses.purpose: To teach mathematical proof making. To teach the concepts of automata, formal languages, and Turing machines. By giving the theoretical grounds in the construction of computers, to ensure the apprehension of the limitations of computing machines.
courses.description: Abstract automata, especially finite state machines; push-down automata; and Turing machines. Formal languages, especially context-free languages. The relationship between automata and languages. Computability and solvability.
learning_outcomes: To be able to classify language definitions.
To be able to analyze problems and provide appropriate representations for them.
To be able to show abstraction ability.
To be able to identify some difficult problems in computer science.
weekly_topics: Hafta 5: Deterministic and Nondeterministic Finite Automata
resources: Lewis, H. R., & Papadimitriou, C. H. (1998). Elements of the Theory of Computation. ACM SIGACT News, 29(3), 62-78.
Sipser, M. (1996). Introduction to the Theory of Computation. ACM Sigact News, 27(1), 27-29.

courses.name: Concepts of Programming Languages
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to teach students the fundamentals of programming languages from different paradigms. Students will be able to understand the concepts of syntax, scope, and binding in the context of programming languages and write programs based on these concepts. Additionally, they will gain knowledge of functional and logic programming languages and learn the basic concepts related to these paradigms through practical applications.
courses.description: In this course, the fundamental concepts of programming languages and different programming paradigms will be covered. Students will gain knowledge in the following areas: Introduction to Programming Languages: The history of programming languages, types, and fundamental features. Programming Language Syntax: The structure of programming languages, syntax rules, and structural elements of a language. Names, Scopes, and Bindings: Relationships between variables, functions, and other constructs, scope concepts, and how bindings work. Control Flow: Conditional statements, loops, and control structures for managing program flow. Functional Languages (Introduction to Scheme): Functional programming paradigm, features of functional languages, and an introduction to the Scheme language. Data Types: Definition of data types, basic data structures, and type conversions. Subroutines and Control Abstraction: Use of subroutines, functions, and procedures, and control abstraction techniques. Logic Languages (Introduction to Prolog): Logic programming paradigm, fundamentals of Prolog, and practical applications. Data Abstraction and Object Orientation: Object-oriented programming, classes, objects, inheritance, and abstraction. Throughout the course, students will be exposed to how various programming languages are used in practice, as well as a comparison of languages from different paradigms. Additionally, essential programming skills, such as understanding the compilation and execution phases of a program and writing high-level functions, will be developed.
learning_outcomes: Being able to use languages belonging to different programming paradigms.
Demonstrating the ability to easily use a new programming language.
Being able to explain the compilation and execution phases of a program.
Being able to write high-level functions.
weekly_topics: Hafta 14: Data Abstraction and Object Orientation
resources: M. L. Scott, Programming Language Pragmatics (4th Edition), Morgan Kaufmann, 2016.
R. W. Sebesta, Concepts of programming languages (11th Edition), Pearson, 2016.

courses.name: Logic Design
courses.purpose: Introduction to computer architecture and the fundamental principles needed to analyze and design digital systems. The course aims to develop the ability to design, test, and apply logic and arithmetic circuits in both theoretical and laboratory settings.
courses.description: The course covers theoretical and experimental studies related to digital logic and computer architecture, including boolean algebra, logic gates, multi-level logic gates, combinational arithmetic and logic systems, sequential logic, registers and counters, memory units, and problem solving with programmable logic.
learning_outcomes: To be able to understand computer architecture and processes.
To be able to understand how arithmetic and logic processing unit works and the ability to design.
To be able to understand memory unit and the ability to understand how it works.
To be able to design circuitry for defined problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Arithmetic operations and base conversions
Hafta 3: Gate circuits and Boolean equations
Hafta 5: Additional gate types and tri state logic
Hafta 6: Design steps, hierarchical design, and technology mapping
Hafta 8: Design with decoders, encoders, multiplexers, demultiplexers
Hafta 9: Binary adders and binary adder-subtractors
Hafta 10: Binary multiplication and design by contraction
Hafta 11: bcd adder, carry lookahead generator, magnitude comparator
resources: Logic and Computer Design Fundamentals, 4th Edition (2007) Prentice – Hall [M. Morris Mano, Charles Kime]
Digital Design, 4th Edition (2007) Prentice – Hall [M. Morris Mano, Michael D. Ciletti]

courses.name: Numerical Computation
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to numerical techniques used in scientific and engineering computation and to develop the ability to choose and apply appropriate numerical algorithms for practical problems.
courses.description: Surveys and applications of numerical techniques related to matrix inversion, systems of linear equations and optimization, finite difference expressions, interpolation and approximation, numerical differentiation and integration. The problems of speed, accuracy and applicability of the topics are examined with related algorithms. The applications of these numerical methods and subjects on computers using efficient programming techniques and with necessary programming languages.
learning_outcomes: To be able to explain effects of finite representation of real numbers for a given algorithm.
To be able to find numerical errors in calculations and compare numerical errors of different algorithms for the same problem.
To be able to solve numerical problems that include derivative, integral, interpolation and/or optimization.
To be able to apply recursive solutions to numerical problems.
To be able to implement linear/non-linear systems for the given problem.
To be able to apply appropriate numerical algorithm for given linear/non-linear system.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to numerical calculation methods
Hafta 8: Finding roots of equations, Newton method
Hafta 9: Finding roots of equations, Secant method
Hafta 10: The solution of simultaneous linear algebraic equations
resources: Numerical Analysis, The new international edition, 2ed, Timothy Sauer
Numerical Algorithms: Methods for Computer Vi- sion, Machine Learning, and Graphics, J. Solomon Matrix Computations (4th Ed.), G. H. Golub and C. F. Van Loan

courses.name: Circuits and Electronics
courses.purpose: To provide students with the fundamental electrical engineering principles and abstractions used in the design of electronic systems. The course aims to enable students to analyze and design simple electronic circuits and to understand the relationship between discrete structures and their digital circuit implementations.
courses.description: Fundamentals of electric circuits, including the basic concepts such as voltage, current, and power; basic components such as resistance, inductance, capacity, MOS transistor, and operational amplifier; basic laws such as Ohm’s law, Kirchhoff’s law, and equivalent resistance exchange; and methods of circuit analysis. The course also covers transient analysis, frequency response, Laplace transforms, Fourier series, introduction to electronic materials and devices, diodes, bipolar transistors and logic families, and MOS technology.
learning_outcomes: To be able to analyse circuits and devise solutions to design problems.
To be able to design and conduct experiments, to be able to interpret the results.
To be able to analyse and use fundamental electronics equipment, to design electronic circuits.
To be able to realize logic gates based the fundamentals of digital electronics.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to electrical circuits and basic components
Hafta 4: Analysis of first order circuits
Hafta 5: Analysis of second order circuits
Hafta 7: Large and small signal models, sinusoidal response
resources: Anant Agarwal, Jeffrey H. Lang Foundations of Analog and Digital Electronic Circuits . 2005. Morgan Kaufmann Pub

courses.name: Analysis and Design of Algorithms
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the theoretical and practical background needed to solve mathematical and computational problems using algorithmic thinking.
courses.description: The course covers the role of algorithms in computing, growth of functions, recurrences, probabilistic analysis and randomized algorithms, dynamic programming, greedy algorithms, advanced data structures, graph algorithms, and NP-completeness.
learning_outcomes: To be able to propose a solution using algorithmic approach
To be able to identify algorithm design alternatives for a given problem
To be able to analyze the complexity of applications
resources: Introduction to Algorithms, 3rd Ed., T. H. Cormen, C. E. Leiserson, R. L. Rivest, C. Stein, MIT Press

courses.name: Probability and Statistics
courses.purpose: This course provides an elementary introduction to probability and statistics with an emphasis on experimentation using computer programming.
courses.description: Topics include permutation, combination, random variables, probability distributions, Bayesian inference, hypothesis testing, and confidence intervals. The course also covers elementary probability theory, conditional probability and independence, distribution functions, joint and conditional distributions, the law of large numbers, the central limit theorem, parameter estimation, and statistical testing.
learning_outcomes: To be able to explain and apply the concepts of probability and random variables.
To be able to describe and use common probability distributions and their properties.
To be able to employ descriptive statistics.
To be able to estimate distribution parameters.
To be able to calculate confidence intervals.
To be able to conduct hypothesis testing.
To be able to utilize computation as a tool to explore concepts in probability and statistics and to do quantitative analysis.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Population and Variates, Tables and Graphs, the Center of a Set of Observations
Hafta 2: The Measure of Variability, Samples and Population
Hafta 4: Random Variables, Expectation of a Random Variable
Hafta 5: Discrete Random Variables, Uniform, Bernoulli, Binomial and Poisson Distibutions
Hafta 6: Continuous Random Variables, Normal Distribution
Hafta 10: Testing Hypotheses, Large Sample Tests
Hafta 11: Testing Hypotheses, Small Sample Tests
Hafta 12: Testing Hypotheses Using X^2 Distibution
resources: Peter Olofsson, Mikael Andersson, Probability, Statistics, and Stochastic Processes, Wiley.
Michael Baron, Probability and Statistics for Computer Scientists, CRC Press.
Allen B. Downey, Think Stats: Probability and Statistics for Programmers, Green Tea Press.

courses.name: Computer Architecture
courses.purpose: To equip students with fundamental knowledge of computer organization and design.
courses.description: Basic computer organization concepts. Instruction set architecture. Assembly-level programming. Processor design. Pipelining. Data and control hazards. Memory hierarchy and cache. Parallelism and system performance.
learning_outcomes: Ability to design general-purpose and application-specific microprocessors
Ability to demonstrate experience in microprocessor implementation and testing
Ability to program in assembly language
resources: Computer Organization and Design: The Hardware/Software Interface (The Morgan Kaufmann Series in Computer Architecture and Design), 5th Edition, by David A. Patterson, John L. Hennessy.

courses.name: Computer Networks
courses.purpose: To equip students with fundamental knowledge of computer networks.
courses.description: Overview of computer networks; packet switching, Internet, layered architecture; network applications; web, email, domain name system; reliable data transfer, congestion and flow control, UDP, TCP; switching, forwarding, routing, IP; medium access control, local area networks, Ethernet.
learning_outcomes: Ability to analyze communication systems using fundamental concepts of communication.
Ability to analyze the layered network model and Internet protocols.
Ability to design networks and protocols.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Overview of Networks (Internet, packet switching)
Hafta 2: Overview of Networks (Routers, layered arhitecture)
Hafta 3: Application Layer (Principles of network applications)
Hafta 8: Transport Layer (Congestion and flow control)
Hafta 11: Network Layer (Software defined networks SDN)
Hafta 12: Data Link Layer (Error control, medium access MAC)
resources: Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach 8th edition, Jim Kurose, Keith Ross, Pearson, 2022.

courses.name: Software Engineering
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with knowledge and practical skills related to software engineering principles and practices. It introduces the software engineer's role, development life cycle models, requirements analysis, system design, verification and validation, software quality metrics, team organization, and human-computer interaction design.
courses.description: The course covers software engineering fundamentals, including software development life cycle models, requirements analysis and specification, system design, software design standards, software testing standards, verification and validation processes, and the use of software quality metrics. It also includes project-based work on requirements analysis, software design documents, test reports, implementation, and team-based development activities.
learning_outcomes: Identify functional requirements for a given project
Distinguish between functional and non-functional requirements
Apply object-oriented design methodology
Prepare a project definition document
Utilize and apply software development process models
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction - Software Development Life Cycle, Team Formation, Course Overview
Hafta 2: Software Design - Basic Design Principles, Software Architecture
Hafta 3: Design Standards - IEEE 1016, formatting rules, templates
Hafta 4: Design Draft Discussion - Group review of initial design submissions
Hafta 5: Design Review Meeting - Group presentations and instructor feedback
Hafta 6: Software Testing - Basic concepts, test design, strategies
Hafta 7: Testing Standards - IEEE 29119 and documentation templates
Hafta 8: Test Report Draft Discussion - Peer review of initial test reports
Hafta 9: Test Report Review Meeting - Improved test strategies discussion
Hafta 11: Updated Reports Discussion - Design & test reports review
Hafta 12: Updated Reports Discussion - Final feedback for improvement
Hafta 13: Final Version Demo - Final delivery of the complete system
Hafta 14: Final Exam - Written exam or project presentation
resources: IEEE STD 1016 – Systems Design—Software Design Descriptions
IEEE STD 29119 – Software and Systems Engineering — Software Testing

courses.name: Human Computer Interaction
courses.purpose: To introduce students with the methods and principles of human factors. To teach the concepts of human computer interaction (HCI) and user-centered design (UCD). To provide students with the ability to make usability evaluation.
courses.description: A review of HCI concepts and history, input devices and interaction techniques, information appliances, user interface design paradigms, 3D user interfaces, adaptive interfaces, user modeling, speech recognition and conversational interfaces, wearable computers, multimodal interfaces, and perceptual interfaces.
learning_outcomes: To be able to gain an understanding of human-computer interaction theory and research.
To be able to recognize the impact of system design on users’ attitudes and behaviors.
To be able to describe user-centered design and evaluation processes.
To be able to employ user-centered design processes in designing and building systems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Limitations of technology. New technologies in HCI.
Hafta 5: Evaluating usability. Predictive and interpretive evaluation.
Hafta 6: User interface design paradigms. Process of interaction design.
Hafta 8: User modeling and the user profile. Adaptive interfaces.
Hafta 10: Speech recognition and conversational interfaces.
Hafta 12: Ubiquitous Computing. HCI Future Directions: Role of AI, networks, etc.
resources: Dix, A., Finlay, J. , Abowd, G., and Beale, R. ;2003; “Human-computer interaction (3rd ed.)”; Prentice Hall.
Preece, J., Rogers, Y., and Sharp, H.;2002; “Interaction design : beyond human-computer interaction”; John Wiley & Sons.
Norman, D.; 1988; “The design of everyday things”; New York: Basic Books.
Carroll, J.M. ;2001; “Human Computer Interaction in the New Millenium”; Prentice Hall.

courses.name: Operating Systems
courses.purpose: The course aims to provide students with a solid understanding of core operating system concepts and the principles behind their implementation. It also helps students develop the ability to reason about process execution, memory management, synchronization, scheduling, and storage-related issues.
courses.description: The course provides the main operating system functionalities and implementation details. It covers basic operating systems concepts, introduces process concepts and process management policies, discusses main memory and virtual memory techniques, and storage and I/O management issues.
learning_outcomes: Analyze and design modern architecture and operating systems by acquiring the basic concepts of operating systems.
Analyze systems that include operating systems and identify related problems.
Compare different solution methods for operating system problems and choose the most appropriate one.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: OS Structures – CH2 / Shell script
Hafta 11: CPU Scheduling – CH5 / Programming Assignment 2 DEMO
Hafta 12: Main Memory – CH9 / Programming Assignment 3 DEMO
resources: A. Silberschatz, P.B. Galvin, G. Gagne: Operating System Concepts, 10th Edition, John Wiley & Sons, 2018.

courses.name: Information Management
courses.purpose: Gain the ability to design, implement, and query a database as a component of an information system. The course also aims to teach methods and techniques that ensure database consistency and help students become effective users of the data management layer of an information system.
courses.description: Introduction to Database Management Systems, architecture of database management systems, relational model, relational database design, relational algebra, SQL (DML), SQL (DDL), normalization, consistency and security, query optimization, concurrency, transaction management, recovery, index and views, and term projects.
learning_outcomes: Capacity to design a relational database
Capacity to create a relational database
Master relational algebra and SQL
Learn concurrency methods, normalization, security and consistency methods in database management systems
Use databases
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Database Management Systems
Hafta 2: Architecture of Database Management Systems
resources: Edward Sciore, Database Design and Implementation, Second Edition, Wiley, 2020.
Raghu Ramakrishnan and Johannes Gehrke, Database Management Systems, McGraw-Hill, 2001, 3rd Edition.
J. Ullman, Principles of Database Systems, Second Edition, Computer Science Press, 1982.
R. Elmasri, S.K. Navathe, Fundamentals of Database Systems, Third Edition, Addison-Wesley, 2000.

courses.name: Professional Issues in Computer Engineering
courses.purpose: To learn ethical concepts in information technologies, to understand concepts about ethical theories, and to learn privacy and property rights issues.
courses.description: Study of the larger ethical and social issues of computing, including the role of a digital society in the modern world. Practical and theoretical applications of computer privacy and security, including network security measures and encryption protocols. Ethical theory and its application to problems in computing. Seminar discussion on value systems, social impact, and human factors, and about use and misuse of computers.
learning_outcomes: Understanding the ethical phenomenon and legal dimensions in information technology.
Understanding of general ethical concepts.
To be able to understand basics of privacy.
To be able to understand basics of property rights.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Introduction to sociotechnical computer ethics (Johnson.1)
Hafta 3: Introduction to ethics (Quinn.2, Johnson.2, Reynolds.1)
Hafta 4: Introduction to ethics (Quinn.2, Johnson.2, Reynolds.1)
Hafta 5: Ethics in ITconfigured societies (Johnson.3, Quinn.3, Reynolds.5,8,9)
Hafta 6: Information flow, privacy and surveillance(Johnson.4, Quinn.5&6, Reynolds.4)
Hafta 8: Digital intellectual property (Johnson.5, Quinn.4, Reynolds.6)
Hafta 13: Professional ethics in computing (Johnson.7, Quinn.9, Reynolds.2,10)
resources: Computer Ethics, Deborah G. Johnson, 4th Edition (2009)
Ethics for the Information Age, Michael J. Quinn, 8th edition (2020)

courses.name: Senior Design Project & Seminar I
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students identify and analyze a problem, define its scope and requirements, and develop a feasible solution. It also supports the development of project planning, teamwork, technical communication, and presentation skills.
courses.description: Writing technical reports, project management and planning, system analysis, requirement analysis, engineering ethics, data design, software design, and presenting a project.
learning_outcomes: To be able to identify problem.
To be able to analyze the problem, to define the scope, constraint, requirements, to be able to design a solution.
To be able to design a solution that is innovative, sustainable and beneficial to the environment and society.
To be able to learn and use new methods and technologies.
To be able to gain teamwork skills.
To be able to present written and oral presentation skills of the project.
To be able to describe the concepts about entrepreneurship and innovation.
resources: CENG 415-416 Bitirme Projeleri Yönergesi
CENG 415 - Proje Konusu Tanımlama Formu

courses.name: Summer Practice
courses.purpose: The purpose of the internship is to enable students to put into practice the theoretical knowledge they have acquired in their field of study. Through this process, students begin to get acquainted with the professional world and start identifying the areas of interest that may shape their future careers. An internship also helps computer engineering students gain knowledge about the structure and management of an organization, as well as the information systems and system analysis conducted within the company.
courses.description: The internship allows students to engage in practical work, understand the overall structure of the firm, and participate in ongoing projects. It must be completed in a minimum of 20 working days, and internship dates must not conflict with academic semester dates or the summer school period. At the end of the internship, students must submit an internship report, a company survey, and a student survey. The internship report accounts for 100% of the grading.
learning_outcomes: LO1. Ability to transfer theoretical knowledge acquired during university education to real-life applications.
LO2. Awareness of the computer engineering profession, including its roles and responsibilities.
LO3. Ability to observe the working environment during the internship and identify a preferred industry sector.
LO4. Ability to develop solutions individually or as part of a team for problems encountered in practice.
LO5. Ability to improve verbal and written communication skills and understand the importance of foreign language proficiency.
LO6. Ability to present acquired knowledge in the form of a formal report.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Internship must be completed in a minimum of 20 working days.
Hafta 2: Internship dates must not conflict with academic semester dates or the summer school period.
resources: Department of Computer Engineering Internship Directive
prerequisites: It is recommended that students complete all coursework up to the end of the third year.

courses.name: Senior Design Project & Seminar II
courses.description: Invited speakers, change management, coding documentation, coding standards, testing, reliability, security, protyping, user manuals, group demos.
learning_outcomes: To be able to identify problem.
To be able to analyze the problem, define the scope, constraint, requirements, and design a solution.
To be able to design a solution that is innovative, sustainable and beneficial to the environment and society.

courses.name: Information Security
courses.purpose: The main goal of this course is to provide students with a background, foundation, and insight into the many dimensions of information security and to support them to understand information security’s importance in our increasingly computer-driven world.
courses.description: Information Security is a comprehensive study of the principles and practices of computer system security including operating system security, network security, software security and web security. Topics include common attacking techniques such as virus, trojan, worms and memory exploits; the formalisms of information security such as the access control and information flow theory; the common security policies such as BLP and Biba model; the basic cryptography, RSA, cryptographic hash function, and password system; network intrusion detection; software security theory; web security; legal and ethical issues in computer security.

Students learn the foundational theories of information security.
Students learn what are the basic principles and techniques when designing a secure system.

Hafta 2: Understanding the Threats Malicious software (Viruses, trojans, rootkits, worms, botnets) Memory exploits (buffer overflow, heap overflow, integer overflow, format string)
Hafta 3: Formalisms Access control theory, access control matrix Information flow
Hafta 4: Policy Security policies Confidentiality policies (BLP model) Integrity policies (Biba, and Clark-Wilson model) Hybrid policies (Chinese Wall model, role-based access control)
Hafta 5: Cryptography I Block and stream ciphers Cryptographic hash functions, Message Authentication Codes (MAC) Public and private key systems
Hafta 6: Cryptography II Message digests. Approximate strength of ciphers Authentication Password system

Hafta 8: Systems Secure design principles (Least-privilege, fail-safe defaults, complete mediation, separation of privilege) TCB and security kernel construction System defense against memory exploits UNIX security and Security-Enhanced Linux (SELinux) Windows security
Hafta 9: Network Security I TCP/IP security issues DNS security issues and defenses
Hafta 10: Network Security II TLS/SSL Network Intrusion detection and prevention systems Firewalls
Hafta 11: Software Security Vulnerability auditing, penetration testing Sandboxing Control flow integrity
Hafta 12: Web Security User authentication, authentication-via-secret and session management Cross Site Scripting, Cross Site Request Forgery, SQL Injection
Hafta 13: Legal and Ethical Issues Cybercrime and computer crime Intellectual property, copyright, patent, trade secret Hacking and intrusion Privacy, identity theft

PaaS topics cover a range of cloud platforms such as Google App Engine, Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure, and others. Topics include the design, development, and implementation of cloud-based mobile applications, web applications, databases, and systems as well as migrating existing software applications to the cloud and working on hybrid solutions. Comparing different parallel programming methodologies and tools, then effectively take a decision which one will be used.

courses.name: Multicore Architectures and Operating Systems

Analyzing the components of the operating systems for multicore architectures.
Analyzing hardware design problems of multicore architectures.
Analyzing the limits of solutions and restrictions of operating systems for different multicore architecture models.

courses.description: In this course, students will gain knowledge in the following areas: Data models and query languages; storage and retrieval; replication and partitioning in distributed data; transactions; challenges of distributed systems, consistency, and consensus in distributed systems; batch and stream processing.

To be able to decide the technologies, techniques, and tools appropriate for a given task

courses.name: NATURAL LANGUAGE UNDERSTANDING AND GENERATION

courses.name: Security of Information and Communication Technologies

Hafta 3: Main Security Functions and Cryptographic Tools
